title: urtica dioica subsp. hispida
intro: Urtica dioica subsp. hispida, also known as the common nettle or stinging nettle, is a subspecies of flowering plant within the nettle family (Urticaceae). This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of arthritis, eczema, and other skin conditions. The health benefits of Urtica dioica subsp. hispida include reducing pain and inflammation, improving digestive health, and supporting immune function. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is also sometimes used in cooking, particularly in the form of nettle soup or as a leafy green vegetable.
